title: eriogonum deflexum subsp. rixfordii
intro: Eriogonum deflexum subsp. rixfordii, also known as Rixford's buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of California and Nevada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Eriogonum deflexum subsp. rixfordii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
